_meta.scope: Per-brand product data: names, categories, active ingredients, and which compliance_rules.json entries a given product is likely to trigger. Not brand voice — see brand_configs.json for that.

tower_28.note: CONFIRMED (10/10 confidence, per official product page and INCI list). Hypochlorous acid is not incidental — it's the primary positioning pillar of this product, with dedicated brand education ("WTF is Hypochlorous Acid?"). Published INCI is exactly these 3 ingredients; the previous actives list (Centella Asiatica, Niacinamide, Green Tea Extract, taken from the API contract's example payload) does not match the real formulation — that example was evidently placeholder/illustrative data, not accurate product truth, and should not be relied on for actual ingredient facts. This confirmation makes 'antimicrobial_ingredient_claims' directly and highly relevant, not speculative: officially substantiated benefits (anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, helps defend against harmful bacteria, helps reduce redness, helps soothe irritation) are safe to draw from, but 'disinfectant'/'sanitizer' framing remains off-limits regardless — true antibacterial properties do not make that specific marketing language legally safe.
officialMarketingClaims: Naturally produced by the body's immune system
Helps defend skin from harmful bacteria

half_magic.brandSpecificClaimToInclude: Medical-grade, irritation-free self-adhesive — stays put all night, no separate lash glue needed.
